title: Cache Performance, Scope, Integrity, and Poisoning Defense · Delivery-System Trace: Ci Cache Artifact Distinction
summary: A privileged release restores an executable cache written from a fork, accepts broad restore keys, hides cold-install failures, and attributes network time to compilation. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci cache artifact distinction increment in cache threat and measurement policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate disposable performance caches from immutable artifacts, job outputs, external registries, and release records by trust and lifetime.
Download by expected run and artifact identity, verify digest and file layout, preserve permissions deliberately, and treat cross-run contents as untrusted.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:

  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example privileged release restores executable cache content written by a fork through a broad fallback key. Compare cold and warm time, key scope, save authority, poisoning risk, and immutable artifact separation. Competency probe: Separate disposable performance caches from immutable artifacts, job outputs, external registries, and release records by trust and lifetime. A defensible solution applies Ci Cache Artifact Distinction, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to cache threat and measurement policy.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-worked-gha-cache-performance-integrity-2
  - competency: ci-worked-gha-cache-performance-integrity-2
    artifact: cache-policy.yaml
    decision: worked gha cache performance integrity 2
    environment: trusted release tag requiring an environment approval
    fault-injection: route five percent traffic to a revision with a data incompatibility
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked2
    change: |
      cache-content: npm-downloads-only
      key: os-arch-node24-lockhash-config
      restore-prefix: exact-trusted-branch
      writer: trusted-push-only
      untrusted-reader: bounded-read
      node-modules: excluded
      privileged-release-cache: disabled
      cold-run: scheduled
      measurement: queue-install-build-test-upload
    verify: compare cold warm and poisoned-key fixtures while checking writer event scope restored key and critical path
    failure: Reject cache as provenance, executable fork-writable caches, broad restore keys, node_modules reuse, or warm-only evidence.
    repair: Cache disposable inputs, bind keys and writers, isolate trust domains, retain cold runs, and measure the actual critical path.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A cache hit proves the restored content belongs to the current revision. This fails the Ci Cache Artifact Distinction contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

# Evidence: ci-cache-artifact-distinction-37pe5g
  - competency: ci-cache-artifact-distinction
    artifact: cache-policy.yaml

    environment: public fork pull request with no repository write access
    fault-injection: change the event from pull_request to pull_request_target
    retained-proof: image digest SBOM provenance issuer workflow builder and subject

    change: |
      cache-content: npm-downloads-only
